videos.title: URGENTE ALAN DOS SANTOS ENTREGA A VERDADE #NotíciasDoBrasil#PolêmicaPolítica#DebatePolítico
videos.description:
Inscreva-se para acompanhar conteúdos sobre:

política brasileira hoje,
notícias políticas atualizadas,
STF e decisões polêmicas,
corrupção no Brasil,
governo Lula e Bolsonaro,
liberdade, democracia e soberania nacional.
Uma grave denúncia está repercutindo nas redes. O jornalista Allan dos Santos, ligado à Revista Timeline, afirmou que teria informações comprometedoras envolvendo o presidente Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva e o ex-ministro José Dirceu.

Segundo o jornalista, as denúncias levantariam questionamentos graves sobre supostos crimes no Brasil e poderiam gerar forte repercussão política caso sejam investigadas.

O caso está gerando grande debate nas redes sociais e dividindo opiniões. Afinal, trata-se de uma denúncia explosiva que, se comprovada, poderia ter grande impacto no cenário político brasileiro.

🛑 Aviso legal:
As opiniões expressas neste vídeo são de responsabilidade do criador de conteúdo, com base na liberdade de expressão garantida pela Constituição. Todo o conteúdo é produzido com fins informativos e de entretenimento.

videos.title: 'MBS FRUSTRADO...': Príncipe saudita ROMPE COM Trump sobre 'erros' na guerra com o Irã? | GRANDE ...
videos.description:
A Arábia Saudita está cada vez mais frustrada com os Estados Unidos devido à forma como o presidente Donald Trump lidou com a guerra contra o Irã. As preocupações em Riad giram em torno dos riscos de escalada, das ameaças à infraestrutura crítica e da incerteza quanto à estratégia americana. Embora publicamente peçam moderação, as autoridades sauditas agora pressionam pela desescalada em meio a temores de um conflito regional crescente.

#saudiarabia #trump #iran #eua #middleeast #geopolitics #gulf #war #conflict #breakingnews

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Saudi Arabia is growing increasingly frustrated with the United States over President Donald Trump's handling of the war with Iran as concerns mount in Riyad over both the conduct of the conflict and the risks it poses to the Gulf. According to analysts and officials cited by Financial Times, the kingdom now has an utter sense of disappointment with Washington, reflecting unease over what is being seen as boratic decision-making and unpredictable messaging from the White House. At the heart of Saudi concerns are Trump's public threats to target Iranian energy infrastructure, including power plants, a move Gulf officials fear could trigger large-scale retaliation across the region. Diplomats from multiple Arab states reportedly reached out urgently to US officials after such remarks, warning that an escalation at that level would unleash missile attacks that regional defenses may not be able to fully absorb. Riyad's discomfort has also been compounded by Trump's suggestion that Gulf countries could be expected to bear the financial cost of the war despite having little control over its trajectory. Analysts say this has reinforced a perception within Saudi leadership that the US is treating regional partners as both stakeholders and pay masters without offering corresponding strategic clarity. The strain has been further aggravated by Trump's remarks about Crown Prince Muhammad bin Salman, including comments that were viewed in Riyad as disparaging and undiplomatic despite earlier praise. For Saudi leadership, such statements are seen not just as rhetorical slips, but as indicators of a broader unpredictability in US engagement. Beyond rhetoric, the war itself has heightened Saudi vulnerabilities. Iran has already demonstrated its ability to strike across the Gulf, targeting energy facilities, desalination plants, and industrial infrastructure in multiple countries. For Saudi Arabia, heavily dependent on energy exports and critical infrastructure, the prospect of sustained attacks raises the risk of severe economic and humanitarian consequences. That damage to desalination systems alone could have devastating effects given the kingdom's reliance on them for water supply. The potential closure of the straight of Hormuz remains another major concern. Trump's suggestion that regional states should take responsibility for reopening the vital shipping lane has added to unease with Gulf officials questioning both the feasibility and the strategic implications of such expectations. At the same time, Saudi Arabia finds itself in a difficult strategic position. While it has long viewed Iran as a regional threat and initially saw some advantage in Thran's military weakening, the evolving nature of the conflict has forced a reassessment. Now the kingdom fears a scenario in which the US either escalates the war unpredictably or withdraws prematurely after declaring victory, leaving behind a more aggressive and emboldened Iran. This dual risk of escalation without control and disengagement without resolution has driven Riyads to prioritize containment and deescalation. Saudi officials have been actively engaging with regional partners including Pakistan, Egypt and Turkey in efforts to push for a diplomatic resolution. There are also growing concerns about secondary escalation fronts. The entry of Iranbacked Houthi forces into the conflict has raised alarms about the security of the Bab al-Mandde Strait, a critical maritime choke point. Any disruption there combined with tensions in the straight of Hormuz could severely impact global trade and energy flows, further amplifying the stakes for Gulf economies. Taken together, the situation reflects a widening gap between Saudi Arabia's strategic priorities and the current trajectory of US policy. Publicly, Riyad continues to call for restraint and deescalation. Privately, however, the message appears clearer. The kingdom is increasingly wary of a conflict that it did not initiate, cannot control, and may ultimately be forced to endure the consequences regardless of how or when it ends. News never stops. The world keeps turning and every turn tells a story. Wars, elections, geopolitics, Davids versus Goliaths. While everyone is celebrating the US China trade tour, Trump has been quietly doing something else across Asia. were once home to the ancient silk road connecting east and west. But who's connecting the dots? Who's decoding the global storm? Welcome to Times of India videos. You're watching a special edition of Global Pulse with me, Niha Kana. We decode the shifting grains of geopolitics to you from Washington to Wuhan. Everything everywhere all at once. Just two years ago, Canada was one of the most popular countries for Indian students. From Davos to Delhi, we track the decisions that move markets, shift policies and shape the future. No jargon, no bias, just clear, credible global journalism. Because understanding the world doesn't have to be complicated. It just has to be smart and that's what we do best. At Times of India videos, we don't just tell stories, we build perspective. We connect the dots between what's global and what's personal. What's happening out there and what it means for you. It's fast. It's fearless. It's making sense of the changing world order. Times of India videos because your world view is just a tap away.

videos.title: 'DIA NEGRO PARA A FORÇA AÉREA DOS EUA': Irã ATINGE 4 aeronaves americanas; F-35 furtivo e Black H...
videos.description:
Dois helicópteros militares dos EUA foram atingidos por fogo iraniano na sexta-feira, enquanto realizavam uma missão de busca e resgate da tripulação de um caça abatido. Um helicóptero UH-60 Black Hawk da Força Aérea dos EUA e um segundo helicóptero de busca e resgate foram atingidos, mas conseguiram escapar, disse ao The Hill uma fonte familiarizada com o assunto, que falou sob condição de anonimato para compartilhar detalhes operacionais. Um assessor do Congresso, também sob condição de anonimato, disse ao The Hill que o Pentágono informou ao Comitê de Serviços Armados da Câmara dos Representantes na sexta-feira que o paradeiro do segundo militar abatido era desconhecido. As forças armadas iranianas anunciaram que suas forças de defesa aérea abateram pelo menos dois caças, cinco drones e mísseis na sexta-feira, descrevendo o ocorrido como um "dia negro" para as forças aéreas americanas e israelenses.

#guerradoIrã #IrãvsEUA #EstadosUnidos #notíciasaovivo #DonaldTrump #ForçasArmadasEUA #Hezbollah #IRGC #Teerã #F35 #Trump

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Two US military helicopters were hit by Iranian fire on Friday while engaging in a search and rescue mission for the crew of a downed fighter jet. A US Air Force UH60 Blackhawk helicopter and a second search and rescue helicopter were struck but were able to escape. A source familiar with the matter speaking on condition of anonymity to share operational matters told the Hill. A congressional aid speaking on the condition of anonymity told the Hill that the Pentagon briefed the House Armed Services Committee on Friday that the status of the second service member is not known. The development follows repeated assertions by President Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegsth that the US has achieved dominance in the skies and significantly weakened Thran's combat capabilities. Iranian armed forces announced that its air defense forces downed at least two fighter jets and five missiles and drones on Friday, describing it as a black day for the American and Israeli air forces. Iranian armed forces have said they shot down a US-made Lucas drone over the Persian Gulf in the latest claim of aerial interceptions amid the ongoing conflict. According to Iranian statements, the drone was detected and engaged by air defense systems before being destroyed. Officials did not specify the exact location of the interception or provide visual confirmation. and the claim has not been independently verified. The Lucas lowcost uncrrewed combat attack system is described as a relatively lowcost combat drone designed for strike missions and is believed to be inspired by loitering munitions concepts used by Iran's Shahed drones. It is intended to provide a more expendable option compared to larger high value UAVs. This marks another instance in which Iran has claimed to target US aerial assets following earlier reports of drone interceptions and shooting down fighter jets in and around its airspace and nearby waters. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States air force capabilities having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15 three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south. F18, one in south. F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims, saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. George has been asked to take early retirement from the post of Army Chief of Staff which he has held since August 2023. The development comes as a new report claimed that Trump administration officials were hiding the data related to US troops wounded or killed in the Middle East. Is the Pentagon carrying out a casualty cover up of US troops killed or wounded in the war against Iran? Is the Trump administration under reporting figures hiding 750 casualties in the Middle East conflict almost 750 American troops have been wounded or killed in the Middle East since October 2023. The Intercept has reported, according to two government officials cited in the report, hundreds of US personnel have been killed or injured in the Middle East since the US launched a war on Iran over a month ago. The government officials told Intercept that 15 US troops were wounded on Friday in an Iranian attack on a Saudi air base that hosts American troops. As of today, there have been eight US military fatalities associated with the war in Iran. How many American deaths are you willing to have in this war war? Well, as I said before, uh when you have conflicts like this, you always have death. And I was at do yesterday. I met the parents and they were unbelievable people. They were unbelievable people. But they all had one thing in common. They said to me one thing, every single one. Finish the job, sir. Please finish the job and I'll leave you at that. Pardon? Pardon me for interrupting. We'll be the US will be gone or done with the war in I think two or three weeks. If it is because there's no reason for us to do this. Look, problem with this trait. A guy can take a mine, drop it in the water and say, "Oh, it's unsafe. It's not like you're taking out an army or you're taking out a country or you." He can drop it. Or you can take a machine gun from the shore and shoot a little few bullets at a ship. Or maybe an over the shoulder missile, small missiles. Uh, that's not for us. That'll be for France. That'll be for whoever is using the trade. But I think when we leave probably that's all cleared up today. I heard tremendous numbers of ships were sailing through. Uh, we're negotiating with them right now. Uh, they've been, again, we have had regime change. Now, regime change was not one of the things I had as a goal. I had one goal. They will have no nuclear weapon. And that goal has been attained. They will not have nuclear weapons. Uh but we're finishing the job and I think within maybe two weeks, maybe a couple of days longer to do the job, but we want to knock out every single thing they have. Now, it's possible that we'll make a deal before that cuz we'll hit bridges and we've hit some we'll hit some bridges. got a couple of nice bridges in mind. Uh but if they come to the table, that'll be good. But doesn't matter whether they come or not. We've set them back. It'll take 15 to 20 years for them to rebuild what we've done to them. According to the Intercept, the US Central Command has allegedly sent outdated statements on casualty numbers. Sentcom has not provided a count of troops who died in the region since the outbreak of the war. An intercept analysis put the number at no less than 15. According to the intercept report, the Pentagon reportedly provided detailed chronologies of attacks on US bases in the Middle East that listed the specific outpost that was attacked, the type of strike, and whether or how many casualties resulted. But the Trump administration's numbers lacked detail and clarity. According to the report, sentcom casualty figures apparently did not include more than 200 soldiers treated for smoke inhalation or otherwise injured due to a fire that raged aboard the USS Gerald Ford before it was forced to head to Suda Bay in Greece for repairs. On Tuesday, US Secretary of War Pete Hedgeth claimed that Iran retained the ability to retaliate for US strikes, but their attacks would be ineffective.

videos.title: 'TOTALMENTE DESTRUÍDO…': Irã CAÇA E INCENDEIA AVIÃO DOS EUA EM PERSEGUIÇÃO AÉREA; VÍDEO DIFÍCIL É...
videos.description:
O Irã afirmou ter abatido um drone de combate LUCAS, de fabricação americana, sobre o Golfo Pérsico, marcando mais uma escalada na dimensão aérea do conflito. O UAV teria sido interceptado por sistemas de defesa aérea, embora não haja verificação independente. O incidente se soma a um padrão crescente de interceptações de drones em um espaço aéreo cada vez mais disputado.

#iran #eua #drone #uav #lucas #golfopérsio #guerra #defesaaérea #geopolítica #conflito #notíciasdeúltimahora

videos.title: Um dos dois tripulantes do F-15E foi resgatado: Coronel aposentado da Força Aérea dos EUA detalha...
videos.description:
Os EUA resgataram um militar de um caça F-15E abatido sobre o Irã, disseram fontes americanas à CNN. A situação do segundo tripulante não estava clara imediatamente após os EUA iniciarem as buscas no país.

O Irã afirmou que suas forças armadas abateram o caça americano. A mídia estatal pediu aos iranianos que ficassem atentos à tripulação americana e prometeu uma recompensa por sua entrega.

0:00 Fontes: 1 de 2 tripulantes resgatados após caça abatido no Irã
0:19 O analista militar da CNN, Coronel da Força Aérea dos EUA Cedric Leighton (aposentado), explica as imagens dos destroços
5:53 O analista militar da CNN, Tenente-General Mark Schwartz (aposentado), comenta o que provavelmente está acontecendo no Quartel-General do CENTCOM
8:44 Schwartz descreve o treinamento de sobrevivência militar
11:23 Trump está exagerando o impacto dos ataques dos EUA no Irã?
13:43 O painel do programa "Inside Politics" analisa a opinião pública sobre a guerra com o Irã.

Assista a mais vídeos da CNN aqui: https://cnn.it/4kh5RPe

#GuerraNoIrã #Caça #Notícias

videos.title: 'Um discurso sem credibilidade': Prof. Sachs
videos.description:
O discurso do presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, sobre o Irã foi repleto de bravatas e não de credibilidade, na opinião de Jeffrey Sachs, diretor do Centro para o Desenvolvimento Sustentável da Universidade de Columbia, em conversa exclusiva com Tian Wei, apresentador da CGTN. Na área energética, ele alertou que um grave choque petrolífero já está em curso e pode desencadear uma enorme crise econômica global. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição This kind of sentiment is so vulgar and so illegal by the way under international law because countries are not governments are not to say these things under the UN charter. They're not even to threaten the use of force much less to use this kind of force. So, Professor Saxs, you and I are talking right now minutes after the US president gave a national address on the latest situation regarding the war in uh Iran. Now, we understand among the facts that he gives, one is the conflict is likely to last two to three more weeks. What does that mean? I don't think anything is really in Donald Trump's control. The speech was a lot of bravado. Uh the speech was a lot of uh delusion in my view. And during the speech uh the price of oil rose $5 a barrel just in the 25 minutes of the speech. Meaning the markets did not find any of it ex very credible. So I think we have to do what we normally have to do with Trump which is filter these remarks. He said America has won uh two more weeks of bombing Iran back to the stone age and then it will go home. Okay. He also said that the US isn't going to open the stray to formoose. Very interesting. They can't. So if the US has won so decisively, why is that little fact left there? Well, I think basically we have a speech with no credibility by a president with no credibility. Now, Professor Sax, let's go on to another question uh that pretty much being mentioned. The US president argued that the US does not need oil coming from the straight of Hormuz. that's not a concern of the United States because the US pretty much can depend on its own oil. Uh is that true? And how much is US likely to be impacted economically speaking by the ongoing conflict? Well, Americans pay the same price of oil as everybody else. There's a world market for oil. Given that there's a world shortage, the prices of oil and all its derivative products and fertilizer and petrochemicals is soaring in the United States. Americans are, of course, feeling it the same way as everybody else. Uh it may be true that some of our oil companies are making a fortune. Some of Trump's friends may be making a fortune, but the American people are suffering along with everybody else. uh his approval rating is plummeting um and it will continue to plummet. He's a a first class fool as far as I'm concerned. Professor, so you are suggesting that yes indeed the US does not depend on oil coming from Iran or the straight of hormones directly. However, the inflation is a problem for the United States given the fact that the straight of hormones is crucial for all kinds of supply chains around the world. Of course, that's supporting the US consumers. Is that your argument? Well, that's the fact that there's been a decline, a very significant decline of the supply of oil on world markets. Yeah. uh American companies are part of that world supply. When uh Americans buy oil, they're buying oil in an integrated world market. If you look at the price in West Texas, which is our benchmark, and the price of oil in the so-called Brent European pricing, they're basically the same. Not exactly, but they're in one market. Yeah. I don't know if Trump understands any of this, but uh Americans understand at every moment they're suffering from a global uh energy supply side shock that will uh cause the United States and the rest of the world to have a very very serious economic contraction. Right? We also noticed that the US leader in the national address which is shi say the first one of such kind since the conflict began uh compared this latest conflict of war to some of the earlier uh conflicts and wars. He compared that to World War I, two, Korea war, Vietnam war, Iraq war, uh, and suggests it's only 32 days compared to years of, um, wars in the earlier cases. Now, your take on that? Well, it all depends on how long it takes to destroy things. Uh, we're very efficient at destroying things right now. uh the next two weeks could destroy a lot of the energy supplies in the Middle East. Uh in which case uh Mr. Trump's argument isn't going to mean very much. Uh two or three weeks we could see the oil and the gas supplies, the pipelines, the refineries, the gas fields, uh the uh uh energy grids blown up across the region. Trump has threatened again tonight to blow up the energy infrastructure of Iran. Despite his claims, Iran can readily hit uh the neighbors and it has ample missiles, probably thousands to hit uh the neighbors and to hit Israel, by the way. So, there's a lot of bravado. He cannot uh process anything other than complete bravado and declarations of ultimate might and ultimate victory. I don't think what we hear tonight is anything about facts. What we hear is spin. Uh what we hear is Trump's state of mind. We heard basically the US will not open the straight of Hormuz and there will be several more weeks of war and during that period the amount of damage that can be done to the world economy and to the Middle East countries is vast. If uh sensitive targets are hit and I expect that they will be hit in Iran, Iran will retaliate and we will read tomorrow or the next day about the uh unprecedented damage to the Emirates or to Saudi Arabia or to another country across the Gulf. And so we should understand that's why the markets gave a thumbs down within minutes of Trump's remarks. They did not find it credible. I do not find any of it credible. Bravado is not the same thing as truth. Right. It's not fair if we don't mention the other side of the conflict uh in this latest war. Of course, we also hear uh in the news uh with a so-called open letter by the current Iranian president Ken. He in an open letter questioned whether the United States truly pursues so-called an American first policy or merely acts as a proxy for Israel. Also in that same letter he talked about uh that the Israel and he talked about Iran is in no way uh thinking about harming the United States which has been the arguments the US politicians made on one of the reasons why this war need to happen. Now, your immediate takeaway on that as well. I'm afraid he probably underestimates how violent the United States actually is because for the last two years at these meetings at the United Nations, he's been saying, "We want peace. We want negotiation." Uh his country's leaders in return have been murdered by Israel and the United States. So, I I feel sad because this is a letter saying to the American people, we have no animus against you. We don't like to be attacked by your government. But the fact of the matter is that um the United States leadership and Israel's leadership are out to kill and destroy as much of Iran as possible. The president said in his speech uh that they would uh destroy Iran and bring it back to the stone age. This kind of sentiment is so vulgar and so illegal by the way under international law because countries are not governments are not to say these things under the UN charter. They're not even to threaten the use of force much less to use this kind of force. So let's move on to some of the key questions related to the latest conflict and war and what does that mean both for the United States the Iran and also uh for the rest of the world. Uh one of the things is the key the straight of hormones. Now we've seen several occasions, Professor Saxs, that the US president suggested it is not the US goal to control the straight of Hormuz or to occupy the straight of Hormos including this time in the national address. Um so what would that mean for the others? uh how is that going to reconcile with the earlier news leaking out suggesting the US will have the option of taking over uh the uh K island uh and also uh control the straight of Hormuz. So what he says is not dispositive of what the United States is going to do. All we can say is the following. If the US government has decided that it's not an objective to open the straight of form, it will remain under Iranian control. It will remain essentially closed. Whatever bombing they do in the next two or three weeks and whatever reciprocal response Iran gives will not open the stray to form moves. And what it would mean if Trump is reflecting true American policy, which I have to caution again, but if he is, what it means is we are in for an completely massive global economic crisis at the hands of Israel and the United States. That's the bottom line. That's why the oil prices jumped within minutes of Trump's opening remarks as soon as he said what he said. Right. The straight will close. What is not going to happen is Europeans or the Emirates or the Saudis opening the straight for moves. It's not within their power. Period. Iran is on the other side. It can shell the tankers and it is ready to sink the tankers that try to go through. And that's been seen since the start of this operation. It was the single most predictable point of this operation from the very beginning. The other thing is regarding the straight of hormones because it is in everybody's interests um and therefore what are likely to be the impacts of this if the straight of horm continue to be closed or if the straight of hormones is going to be choked either by the United States or by Iran for the others. Professor Sachs it it won't be choked by the United States. It will be closed by Iran as Iran fights for its survival. That's what will happen. And if that happens, there will be a worldwide economic crisis. But given that, what's also true and important to understand is if this war goes on even for weeks, there will be so much destruction of the oil and gas fields and infrastructure, right? That it won't be the straight of Hormuz essentially that's issue. It will be the physical production in the Middle East that will be at issue. In other words, we are going to have a major energy supply reduction. Trump's speech tonight, again, if it can be taken uh at its word, has essentially made clear there will be a phenomenally sharp, probably unprecedented energy crisis. And that we have every reason to expect will create a worldwide economic crisis. Professor, about the oil prices, we have seen the brand price going up to 150 US, which is the highest even higher than the first Gulf War. Uh meanwhile, we also see countries particularly developing and emerging economies cannot afford to have large storage. Some of them even purchase oil on a daily basis. Um so what kind of da disaster we're likely to see for humans on this earth? We've seen uh oil supply shocks twice in the 1970s and the effects were extremely negative, very severe, sharp declines of uh living standards, uh food supply disruptions and this will be no exception. There will be a lot of suffering by people all over the world care of Israel and the United States. This is what is underway. I heard nothing in the speech that gave me much solace about this. the bravado that two or three weeks more we bomb them to the stone age and it's over and we go home and others can clean up our mess is not a uh cogent approach to what is a an a very deep crisis given I I should add something that may seem a little uh subtle or behind beside the point but I it could be very serious There are projections that the weather in the second half of this year especially I mean the climate because of possible serious El Nino uh which is hugely disruptive of food supplies of rainfall patterns of drought right if that comes and compounds this we will have an absolutely unprecedented crisis on our hands right so this the IMF is already giving reports of this. The International Energy Agency has said that the extent of the energy supply shock is unprecedented. Uh and we heard nothing tonight of any intelligent response to this. Not even a concern about it, frankly. Related to that, we've been hearing an orchestra of different voices coming from the region and also from other parts of world whose interests obviously are directly impacted. We've been hearing from the Gulf States. Some have been calling on the United States and Israel to go all the way so to speak while others have been expressing concerns about the latest situations especially the impacts on the region now and in the future. Meanwhile, we've also been hearing from some European governments uh talking about this is quote not their wars and therefore they are not going to be involved. Some of them refused to open the airspace for the US and Israel for their attacks against Iran. Some of them have been in according to media reports in negotiations with Iran regarding the street of Hormuz. But there are a lot of different voices on so many different issues and actually looking at the war in different directions. Now, Professor Saxs, what would this mean? Is the US purposefully pushing everybody into a corner? Uh if they if it is, what does that mean for everybody else? Is this a critical moment for people to choose what kind of world they want? A lot depends on the truth about the military situation. Yeah. The US and Israel say that they have defeated Iran for all intents and purposes, but the skies are open. It's a cleaning up operation and Iran will be back in the stone age. That is their claim. There is another very widespread view that Iran has thousands of missiles and uh much greater numbers of drones uh that it has not been seriously degraded. The US position is that the uh enriched uranium is buried under the ground and monitored by US satellite and no uh issue there. Uh many other people think this is completely preposterous that the Iranians even before the attack last summer remove the enriched uranium to new locations. And I don't know because I'm not in the information chain, but I could say that a lot of the issues that we're discussing depend on the military assessment. Stepping back, I can only make a couple of general points which I would like to make. First, I have never known the US government to tell the truth about the military situation during my lifetime, whether it is Vietnam or whether it is any of the other wars. So, I don't believe a word that is said until it's shown. I do not take the word of the US government at all straightforwardly. Second, a basic principle of war of the last 100 years is that aerial attacks are not sufficient, especially for a country of nearly 100 million people and a land area which is enormous and mountainous and protected and a regime that has known all along that it has two great enemies, the United States and Israel. It has not been the case that a few weeks of bombing has ever ended a war like this. So on general principles though I am not any kind of definitive expert and I have zero inside information on this. I do not believe the US narrative and many of the things that we're discussing will come down to who is right and wrong about the military situation. I think it is likely that Iran has very substantial military power, missile systems, hypersonic missile systems, the ability to cause grave damage across the Gulf and in Israel. Yeah. And that the US claim that this is not the case is will be proven to be false. If that is right, we are in a completely new world situation. If that conclusion is correct, if the United States is right in what President Trump said, then Iran will have been brutally subjected to aggression, but it will not change fundamentally the world balance. So unfortunately there is no answer right now. Right. uh that that is clear. I believe that the weight of the evidence right now is that uh Trump is uh blowing a lot of hot air uh and as usual the United States government is not being straightforward about the real situation.

videos.title: 'Não esperem por uma guerra mundial. Salvem a ONU agora': alerta o Prof. Sachs.
videos.description:
O professor Jeffrey Sachs, da Universidade Columbia, argumenta que a ordem mundial atual é movida menos por regras do que pelo poder bruto. Em sua visão, o que existe é, na prática, um império americano – um império que não respeita mais o direito internacional. Ele alerta que as Nações Unidas enfrentam desafios crescentes, já que seu país fundador se afastou consideravelmente da organização. Sua mensagem para o resto do mundo é clara: não esperem por outra guerra mundial e ajam agora para preservar a ONU. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs #ONU

📺 Inscreva-se no nosso canal do YouTube e fique por dentro das nossas análises e entrevistas mais recentes: 👇
   / @cgtn   👈

Baixe nosso aplicativo no Google Play (Android): https://play.google.com/store/apps/de...

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição The United States is an empire. It has its empire is reflected in the military bases around the world in Asia, in Korea, in Japan. They've been there for 80 years after all. 80 years over the past decades about the US being the leader of the free world. Now, of course, that is a political slogan. Some can even say it. It is really a propaganda slogan. But on the other hand, is there something called the free world? No. There is something called the world in which America has military bases. There are about 80 countries in the world where the United States has military bases. That is the American Empire. And there are roughly 750 US military bases in around 80 countries. And those countries don't really breathe. When you host an American military base, you give up your sovereignty. That's what's happened to Bahrain, to the Emirates, to Qatar, to Saudi Arabia. And they did that because they thought that is protection for them. It's not protection. It is occupation. And so we don't have clear thought in any part of the world but free world well freedom of US bombing missions that's the freedom uh freedom to break any international law but these slogans mean nothing right now they don't characterize governments they don't characterize behavior they don't care characterize lawfulness I do a study each year on which countries are most aligned with the UN charter. There are 193 UN member states. The United States comes out last on objective criteria. Last. Not at the bottom. Not in the bottom 10%. Not in the bottom 20%. 193rd. The US obeys no international law right now. So what kind of order are we talking about? What we're talking about is force. The United States is an empire. It has its empire is reflected in the military bases around the world in Asia, in Korea, in Japan. They've been there for 80 years after all in 80 years. So that's the empire. When you are betting on that empire, you're betting on the US side of things. The fact of the matter is from the point of view of technology and innovation and markets and trade that world doesn't even exist anymore. Right? The BRICS country, the BRICS countries are much larger than the G7 countries in economic size. China is ahead of the United States in innovation across huge swaths of the economy right now. So still the bases are there and when you watch government behavior of governments that host US military bases it's a little bit like vantriloquism. to hear the US words coming out of the mouths of the leaders of these countries because to host a US military base is to give up your sovereignty to an important right the meanwhile I would like to follow up on a few points professor Saxs you just made I I would love to hear more of your in-depth uh analysis uh to say the least the US role has been recalibrated in terms of global governance that's for sure uh to what degree Well, we're going to observe over the next few weeks and months probably uh even more clearly as you argued. But professor, the US did help to create and reinforce over the past the decades since second world war some of the international institutions the United Nations, IMF, Breton Wood, NATO and some of the list goes on. So how much do you think the value of this different entities, mechanisms and structures should be devalued in a way as a result of the US which is one of the key creators of this system are not believing in this system to begin with in your argument and now certainly want to leave all these international governance structures. So um how much do we still need to value these structures? That's an interesting question, isn't it? And a and a logical question. And my recommendation for the rest of the world, which is somewhat in shock about this, is don't wait for World War II to create a new organization. Save this one. Yeah. And my recommendation for China, very strong recommendation is make a major UN campus in Beijing because there is no major UN organization in Asia. It was born in the United States or in Europe. This is where almost all of the UN agencies are. But Asia is home to more than it's 60% of the world population. True. Half the world output. And so we need to support the UN. You know, the UN is the second attempt. The League of Nations was the first attempt. It failed. It failed and it died in World War II. Now we have the UN. It's hanging by a thread because the host country, the inventor of it, has abandoned it to a large extent. And so the other 190 plus countries need to ask, what are we going to do? Let the United States flush this down the drain or save it. My advice is to save it because World War II is not an option for us. It is not the right approach to say we'll get the next one after the next world war just like the past one. We need to save this. And we desperately need leaders to be saying to rogue nations, and we have two of them right now, the United States and Israel, you cannot do what you're doing. A president of the United States cannot give a speech declaring that they're going to bomb Iran back to the stone age. This has to be unacceptable on its face. It has to have a reaction from governments immediately. Not hm wonder if he's going to bomb them back to the stone age, but rather that is completely unacceptable in the 21st century in a nuclear age. We cannot even have that kind of talk much less that kind of intention. Right. Professor, my last questions related to uh the fear that some have been suggesting if things are not being managed well regarding the conflict among Israel, United States and Iran. Of course, rippling effect, we are likely to be on the verge of a another world war. Well, that is maybe an exaggeration or it might be a clear warning. Uh what do you make of the latest situation and how much uh roles others could play in prevent the things from further escalating into something that nobody wants to see. We're on the verge of world war because of many dangerous delusional ideas right now mainly in the United States. The US has essentially declared it runs the Western Hemisphere. It says that's the Monroe Doctrine. The Monroe Doctrine had nothing to do with that. The Monroe Doctrine was only a warning to Europe. Do not colonize the Western Hemisphere. It was not a claim by the United States to dominate the Western Hemisphere. So the mindset right now is very very dangerous and the mindset of the US is extremely dangerous because it's built on a fundamental delusion that the United States can do what it wants. But if we continue this way there will be wars breaking out everywhere. And interestingly, by the way, I think it's important to say with all the US attack on the evil of Iran, how many wars has Iran launched in the last decade? Zero. In the last 40 years, zero. In the last 80 years, zero. In the last century, zero. Iran has not been a country launching wars. How many has the United States launched? Nearly every year some kind of new military adventure. How do you see this part of the world? Well, you have a conflict going on and it's ripple effect likely to show the true colors of a lot of logics in the world and likely to change the world to certain extent. You also have other players interacting with the United States in different ways. Um, and China, United States is only just one of those example. You also had the EU or the Gulf regions and Latin America and the list goes on. So, how do you see this many moving factors? Uh, what kind of momentum do you read out of this? Well, you know, chi China has a very stable, mature and careful state craft. So uh the Chinese government doesn't see any reason to provoke the United States in areas of trade uh finance uh and related topics and so it's trying to have uh some kind of stability. We saw that in those areas too however Trump had his reciprocal tariffs and his trade wars and his threats and uh everything else. So there's no real stability there as well. As I read the situation, uh, China and, uh, others are trying to have as, uh, non-provocative relations as possible in these areas, but without illusions. Uh, not believing the United States is a stable country or even or much less a stable partner. To believe so would be a huge mistake. China's stable partners are especially the emerging economies of the world. They need what China has. China trades with them. China has good diplomacy with them. And I think the idea is don't rock the boat for no reason. But the truth of the matter is it's also not possible to duck the war and peace issues. China has an enormous role and stake in ending this war in the Gulf just as one of the world's great powers and one of the world's responsible powers. Uh and of course a leader in the United Nations, Russia has the same responsibility. Uh India though it's not a UN security council member has a very big responsibility. So in addition to trying to have uh a state visit perhaps and uh discussions about trade that that don't blow up, the truth of the matter is uh the major powers and I'm again always coming back to China, to Russia, to the BRICS countries really do need to help explain to Mr. Trump and to the United States. You cannot have wars to bomb countries to the stone age in the 21st century. The idea, the approach, the rhetoric can get us all killed and it has to stop. Do you see the danger about future attacks on Cuba or Greenland? Of course. And the list goes on. Of course. Of course. They're absolutely going to invade Cuba one day. They're going to declare Greenland part of US territory one day. This is the mindset right now. Grab what you can. They're absolutely going to do this. Jeffrey Sachs, professor, always a pleasure. Thank you so much for your straightforwardness and insightful ideas. Appreciate it. Great to be with you. Thank you.

videos.title: Irã divulga imagens bombásticas de helicóptero CH-47 Chinook americano destruído após impacto dir...
videos.description:
A mídia estatal iraniana divulgou imagens não verificadas alegando que um ataque com drone danificou um helicóptero CH-47 Chinook dos EUA no Kuwait. Relatos sugerem que a aeronave fazia parte de uma missão relacionada à queda de um caça F-15E Strike Eagle dos EUA em território iraniano. Um membro da tripulação teria sido resgatado, enquanto outro permanece desaparecido, e as operações de busca continuam. O Pentágono não confirmou as alegações. Com o aumento das tensões, o presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, classificou a situação como "guerra", sinalizando que não haverá trégua nas hostilidades. Os acontecimentos representam uma escalada perigosa no conflito em curso entre Irã e EUA.

#GuerraIrãEUA #AtaqueAoChinook #F15Abatido #ÚltimasNotícias #ConflitoNoOrienteMédio #ForçasMilitarEUA #AtaqueAoIrã #AlertaDeGuerra #Geopolítica #Trump

O Times of India (TOI) é o jornal diário em inglês mais vendido do mundo.

Os vídeos do Times of India trazem notícias globais, opiniões e análises precisas. Acompanhamos a ascensão global da Índia, seu crescente engajamento com o mundo, o cenário geopolítico em transformação em meio a conflitos e guerras e a ordem mundial emergente.

NOTÍCIAS INTERNACIONAIS | CONFLITOS GLOBAIS | GUERRA NO ORIENTE MÉDIO | MUDANÇAS NA ORDEM MUNDIAL #TOILive | #TOIVideos

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A dramatic escalation in the Iran US conflict. Unverified images now claim a direct Iranian strike on a US military helicopter in Q8. Iranian state media outlet Press TV has released these images purportedly showing a damaged US Army CH47 Shinook at Camp Buring, a key American military hub in the Gulf. According to Iranian reports, a drone strike hit the aircraft, leaving the heavy lift helicopter severely damaged. The Shinook is a critical asset used to move troops, weapons, and supplies across the region. But there is a major catch. There is no official confirmation yet from Washington. The Pentagon has remained silent so far. Another Iranian outlet claims something even more striking. It says the helicopter was part of a search operation looking for a downed US fighter jet pilot. Because behind this incident lies an even bigger story. A US F-15E Strike Eagle has been shot down over Iran, marking a major turning point in the conflict. According to reports, one crew member has been rescued alive and now in US custody, receiving medical treatment, but the second crew member is still missing. Search and rescue operations are ongoing and time is critical. Meanwhile, tensions continue to surge. US President Donald Trump has responded bluntly, saying the loss of the aircraft will not affect diplomacy. His words were direct. This is war. Trump has also declined to reveal how the US would respond if the missing crew member is captured or harmed. That silence is raising new questions about what comes next. A major development is now emerging from the Iran war, marking a significant escalation in direct confrontation between the United States and Iran. According to American media, it is now confirmed that a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. US special forces have located one of the crew members. Multiple reports quoting sources say the aircraft involved was an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin seat fighter jet used for both airtoair and airto ground missions. The jet was carrying two crew members, a pilot and a weapons systems officer. Reportedly, both crew members successfully ejected after the aircraft was hit by Iranian fire. While one person was rescued alive from inside Iranian territory, search operations are still ongoing for the second crew member. In a significant operational decision, Israel reportedly cancelled planned strikes inside Iran to avoid interfering with the ongoing rescue mission. Iranian state media was the first to report that the US jet had been shot down, though it claimed the aircraft was F-35. State television in Iran also broadcast a message saying that anyone who locates the US personnel would be rewarded by the government. This marks the first confirmed instance since the start of the war that a US fighter jet has been downed by Iran's fire. The White House has acknowledged the development. Press Secretary Caroline Levit told Axios that the president has been briefed on the situation. The F-15E Strike Eagle manufactured by Boeing is a key component of US air power. It is designed to carry out deep strike missions as well as engage in aerial combat. This incident comes amid mounting losses in the conflict. Earlier in the war, three F-15E aircraft were reportedly lost due to friendly fire. The human cost is also rising. At least 13 American personnel have been killed since the start of the campaign. This comes after Donald Trump and his defense secretary Pete Hegseath have both repeatedly suggested that the US has effectively won this war. Hegsathth and military commanders have spoken of air superiority and how degraded Iran's air defenses are. This shows that Iran still retains capacity to defend its skies. As the search continues for the missing crew member and tensions remain at a peak, this latest incident underscores the growing risks facing US forces in an increasingly dangerous and expanding conflict. Iran downs US fighter jet captures its pilot amid war. US military's frantic search for captured pilot fails. Choppers deployed to trace missing F-35 fighter pilot. After Iran shot down a US F-35 fighter jet on Friday, a video circulated on social media showing American helicopters and planes searching for the missing pilot in southern Iran's Kistan. Iranian media also urged residents to capture the pilot dead or alive for a promising reward. Given that the American F-35 fighter J of the Zionist regime, the provincial police force issued an urgent statement announcing that an American Zionist enemy F-35 fighter jet had been targeted and subsequently shot down within the nation's airspace in the central region of the country. And given the potential for the pilot or pilots of this fighter jet to land in Kyiluier and Buoyer Ahmmed province, we earnestly request all members of the general public, including our esteemed nomadic communities and villagers to extend their full cooperation to the military and law enforcement agencies. To the esteemed residents of Kiluya and Buer Ahmad province, you, our beloved, honorable and fiercely patriotic people, I urge you, should you manage to apprehend any enemy pilot or pilots and successfully deliver them alive and unharmed into the custody of our military and law enforcement agencies, you will receive a valuable reward and a bonus for good news. Iranian IRGC linked Tasnim news agency claimed that Iranian forces foiled the US military's bid to rescue the downed fighter jet pilot. Iran's farce news agency shared video showing a purported missile attack on an F-15 air base in Israel's Hifa. The Iranian air defense systems also claimed to have successfully downed an American UH60 Blackhawk helicopter in Iraqi airspace, but the reports remain unverified. Later, Iran's Tasnim news agency claimed that the US military was trying to extract the fighter jet pilot whose condition remained unclear. The operation involved Blackhawk helicopters and a C130 Hercules aircraft. However, their efforts have so far been futile, the agency said. Washington has not commented on any of the claims regarding the alleged downing of a jet or the rescue mission. In March, an American F-35 had to execute an emergency landing after being struck by Iranian fire. Iran has been carrying out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military installations in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday vowed to crush Iran. Together with our American friends, we are continuing to dismantle the terror regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders. We are bombing uh bridges and we are striking their infrastructure. In recent days, our air force has successfully destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This is a truly significant achievement that effectively deprivives the revolutionary guards of both crucial financial resources and their capability to manufacture uh numerous weapons. In in full coordination between me and President Trump, between the Israel Defense Forces and the United States Army, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever. Israel is stronger than ever. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States air force capabilities, having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15, three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south, F18, one in south, F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. George has been asked to take early retirement from the post of Army Chief of Staff which he has held since August 2023. The development comes as a new report claimed that Trump administration officials were hiding the data related to US troops wounded or killed in the Middle East.

videos.title: 'DIA NEGRO PARA A FORÇA AÉREA DOS EUA': Irã ATINGE 4 aeronaves americanas; F-35 furtivo e Black H...
videos.description:
Dois helicópteros militares dos EUA foram atingidos por fogo iraniano na sexta-feira, enquanto realizavam uma missão de busca e resgate da tripulação de um caça abatido. Um helicóptero UH-60 Black Hawk da Força Aérea dos EUA e um segundo helicóptero de busca e resgate foram atingidos, mas conseguiram escapar, disse ao The Hill uma fonte familiarizada com o assunto, que falou sob condição de anonimato para compartilhar detalhes operacionais. Um assessor do Congresso, também sob condição de anonimato, disse ao The Hill que o Pentágono informou ao Comitê de Serviços Armados da Câmara dos Representantes na sexta-feira que o paradeiro do segundo militar abatido era desconhecido. As forças armadas iranianas anunciaram que suas forças de defesa aérea abateram pelo menos dois caças, cinco drones e mísseis na sexta-feira, descrevendo o ocorrido como um "dia negro" para as forças aéreas americanas e israelenses.

#guerradoIrã #IrãvsEUA #EstadosUnidos #notíciasaovivo #DonaldTrump #ForçasArmadasEUA #Hezbollah #IRGC #Teerã #F35 #Trump

We'll be the US will be gone or done with the war in I think two or three weeks. If it is because there's no reason for us to do this. Look, problem with this trait. A guy can take a mine, drop it in the water and say, "Oh, it's unsafe. It's not like you're taking out an army or you're taking out a country or you." He can drop it. Or you can take a machine gun from the shore and shoot a little few bullets at a ship. Or maybe an over the shoulder missile, small missiles. Uh, that's not for us. That'll be for France. That'll be for whoever is using the trade. But I think when we leave probably that's all cleared up today. I heard tremendous numbers of ships were sailing through. Uh, we're negotiating with them right now. Uh, they've been, again, we have had regime change. Now, regime change was not one of the things I had as a goal. I had one goal. They will have no nuclear weapon. And that goal has been attained. They will not have nuclear weapons. Uh but we're finishing the job and I think within maybe two weeks, maybe a couple of days longer to do the job, but we want to knock out every single thing they have. Now, it's possible that we'll make a deal before that cuz we'll hit bridges and we've hit some we'll hit some bridges. got a couple of nice bridges in mind. Uh but if they come to the table, that'll be good. But doesn't matter whether they come or not. We've set them back. It'll take 15 to 20 years for them to rebuild what we've done to them. According to the Intercept, the US Central Command has allegedly sent outdated statements on casualty numbers. Sentcom has not provided a count of troops who died in the region since the outbreak of the war. An intercept analysis put the number at no less than 15. According to the intercept report, the Pentagon reportedly provided detailed chronologies of attacks on US bases in the Middle East that listed the specific outpost that was attacked, the type of strike, and whether or how many casualties resulted. But the Trump administration's numbers lacked detail and clarity. According to the report, sentcom casualty figures apparently did not include more than 200 soldiers treated for smoke inhalation or otherwise injured due to a fire that raged aboard the USS Gerald Ford before it was forced to head to Suda Bay in Greece for repairs. videos.title: 'TOTALMENTE DESTRUÍDO…': Irã CAÇA E INCENDEIA AVIÃO DOS EUA EM PERSEGUIÇÃO AÉREA; VÍDEO DIFÍCIL É...
videos.description:
O Irã afirmou ter abatido um drone de combate LUCAS, de fabricação americana, sobre o Golfo Pérsico, marcando mais uma escalada na dimensão aérea do conflito. O UAV teria sido interceptado por sistemas de defesa aérea, embora não haja verificação independente. O incidente se soma a um padrão crescente de interceptações de drones em um espaço aéreo cada vez mais disputado.

videos.title: Um dos dois tripulantes do F-15E foi resgatado: Coronel aposentado da Força Aérea dos EUA detalha...
videos.description:
Os EUA resgataram um militar de um caça F-15E abatido sobre o Irã, disseram fontes americanas à CNN. A situação do segundo tripulante não estava clara imediatamente após os EUA iniciarem as buscas no país.

O Irã afirmou que suas forças armadas abateram o caça americano. A mídia estatal pediu aos iranianos que ficassem atentos à tripulação americana e prometeu uma recompensa por sua entrega.

0:00 Fontes: 1 de 2 tripulantes resgatados após caça abatido no Irã
0:19 O analista militar da CNN, Coronel da Força Aérea dos EUA Cedric Leighton (aposentado), explica as imagens dos destroços
5:53 O analista militar da CNN, Tenente-General Mark Schwartz (aposentado), comenta o que provavelmente está acontecendo no Quartel-General do CENTCOM
8:44 Schwartz descreve o treinamento de sobrevivência militar
11:23 Trump está exagerando o impacto dos ataques dos EUA no Irã?
13:43 O painel do programa "Inside Politics" analisa a opinião pública sobre a guerra com o Irã.

Assista a mais vídeos da CNN aqui: https://cnn.it/4kh5RPe

#GuerraNoIrã #Caça #Notícias

videos.title: 'Um discurso sem credibilidade': Prof. Sachs
videos.description:
O discurso do presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, sobre o Irã foi repleto de bravatas e não de credibilidade, na opinião de Jeffrey Sachs, diretor do Centro para o Desenvolvimento Sustentável da Universidade de Columbia, em conversa exclusiva com Tian Wei, apresentador da CGTN. Na área energética, ele alertou que um grave choque petrolífero já está em curso e pode desencadear uma enorme crise econômica global. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs

📺 Inscreva-se no nosso canal do YouTube e fique por dentro das nossas análises e entrevistas mais recentes: 👇
   / @cgtn   👈

Baixe nosso aplicativo na Apple Store (iOS): https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/cctvn...
Baixe nosso aplicativo no Google Play (Android): https://play.google.com/store/apps/de...

Para mais informações:
https://www.cgtn.com/video

Stepping back, I can only make a couple of general points which I would like to make. First, I have never known the US government to tell the truth about the military situation during my lifetime, whether it is Vietnam or whether it is any of the other wars. So, I don't believe a word that is said until it's shown. I do not take the word of the US government at all straightforwardly. Second, a basic principle of war of the last 100 years is that aerial attacks are not sufficient, especially for a country of nearly 100 million people and a land area which is enormous and mountainous and protected and a regime that has known all along that it has two great enemies, the United States and Israel. It has not been the case that a few weeks of bombing has ever ended a war like this. So on general principles though I am not any kind of definitive expert and I have zero inside information on this. I do not believe the US narrative and many of the things that we're discussing will come down to who is right and wrong about the military situation. I think it is likely that Iran has very substantial military power, missile systems, hypersonic missile systems, the ability to cause grave damage across the Gulf and in Israel. Yeah. And that the US claim that this is not the case is will be proven to be false. If that is right, we are in a completely new world situation. If that conclusion is correct, if the United States is right in what President Trump said, then Iran will have been brutally subjected to aggression, but it will not change fundamentally the world balance. So unfortunately there is no answer right now. Right. uh that that is clear. I believe that the weight of the evidence right now is that uh Trump is uh blowing a lot of hot air uh and as usual the United States government is not being straightforward about the real situation.

videos.title: 'Não esperem por uma guerra mundial. Salvem a ONU agora': alerta o Prof. Sachs.
videos.description:
O professor Jeffrey Sachs, da Universidade Columbia, argumenta que a ordem mundial atual é movida menos por regras do que pelo poder bruto. Em sua visão, o que existe é, na prática, um império americano – um império que não respeita mais o direito internacional. Ele alerta que as Nações Unidas enfrentam desafios crescentes, já que seu país fundador se afastou consideravelmente da organização. Sua mensagem para o resto do mundo é clara: não esperem por outra guerra mundial e ajam agora para preservar a ONU. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs #ONU

And those countries don't really breathe. When you host an American military base, you give up your sovereignty. That's what's happened to Bahrain, to the Emirates, to Qatar, to Saudi Arabia. There are 193 UN member states. The United States comes out last on objective criteria. Last. Not at the bottom. Not in the bottom 10%. Not in the bottom 20%. 193rd. The US obeys no international law right now. So what kind of order are we talking about? What we're talking about is force. The United States is an empire. It has its empire is reflected in the military bases around the world in Asia, in Korea, in Japan. They've been there for 80 years after all in 80 years. So that's the empire. When you are betting on that empire, you're betting on the US side of things. The fact of the matter is from the point of view of technology and innovation and markets and trade that world doesn't even exist anymore. Right? The BRICS country, the BRICS countries are much larger than the G7 countries in economic size. China is ahead of the United States in innovation across huge swaths of the economy right now. But professor, the US did help to create and reinforce over the past the decades since second world war some of the international institutions the United Nations, IMF, Breton Wood, NATO and some of the list goes on. So how much do you think the value of this different entities, mechanisms and structures should be devalued in a way as a result of the US which is one of the key creators of this system are not believing in this system to begin with in your argument and now certainly want to leave all these international governance structures. So um how much do we still need to value these structures? That's an interesting question, isn't it? And a and a logical question. And my recommendation for the rest of the world, which is somewhat in shock about this, is don't wait for World War II to create a new organization. Save this one. Yeah. And my recommendation for China, very strong recommendation is make a major UN campus in Beijing because there is no major UN organization in Asia. It was born in the United States or in Europe. This is where almost all of the UN agencies are. But Asia is home to more than it's 60% of the world population. True. Half the world output. And so we need to support the UN. You know, the UN is the second attempt. The League of Nations was the first attempt. It failed. It failed and it died in World War II. Now we have the UN. It's hanging by a thread because the host country, the inventor of it, has abandoned it to a large extent. And so the other 190 plus countries need to ask, what are we going to do? Let the United States flush this down the drain or save it. My advice is to save it because World War II is not an option for us. It is not the right approach to say we'll get the next one after the next world war just like the past one. We need to save this. And we desperately need leaders to be saying to rogue nations, and we have two of them right now, the United States and Israel, you cannot do what you're doing. A president of the United States cannot give a speech declaring that they're going to bomb Iran back to the stone age. This has to be unacceptable on its face. It has to have a reaction from governments immediately. Not hm wonder if he's going to bomb them back to the stone age, but rather that is completely unacceptable in the 21st century in a nuclear age. We cannot even have that kind of talk much less that kind of intention. Right. Professor, my last questions related to uh the fear that some have been suggesting if things are not being managed well regarding the conflict among Israel, United States and Iran. Of course, rippling effect, we are likely to be on the verge of a another world war. Well, that is maybe an exaggeration or it might be a clear warning. Uh what do you make of the latest situation and how much uh roles others could play in prevent the things from further escalating into something that nobody wants to see. We're on the verge of world war because of many dangerous delusional ideas right now mainly in the United States. The US has essentially declared it runs the Western Hemisphere. It says that's the Monroe Doctrine. The Monroe Doctrine had nothing to do with that. The Monroe Doctrine was only a warning to Europe. Do not colonize the Western Hemisphere. It was not a claim by the United States to dominate the Western Hemisphere. So the mindset right now is very very dangerous and the mindset of the US is extremely dangerous because it's built on a fundamental delusion that the United States can do what it wants. But if we continue this way there will be wars breaking out everywhere. And interestingly, by the way, I think it's important to say with all the US attack on the evil of Iran, how many wars has Iran launched in the last decade? Zero. In the last 40 years, zero. In the last 80 years, zero. In the last century, zero. Iran has not been a country launching wars. How many has the United States launched? Nearly every year some kind of new military adventure. How do you see this part of the world? Well, you have a conflict going on and it's ripple effect likely to show the true colors of a lot of logics in the world and likely to change the world to certain extent. You also have other players interacting with the United States in different ways. Um, and China, United States is only just one of those example. Well, you know, chi China has a very stable, mature and careful state craft. So uh the Chinese government doesn't see any reason to provoke the United States in areas of trade uh finance uh and related topics and so it's trying to have uh some kind of stability. We saw that in those areas too however Trump had his reciprocal tariffs and his trade wars and his threats and uh everything else. So there's no real stability there as well. You cannot have wars to bomb countries to the stone age in the 21st century. The idea, the approach, the rhetoric can get us all killed and it has to stop. Do you see the danger about future attacks on Cuba or Greenland? Of course. And the list goes on. Of course. Of course. They're absolutely going to invade Cuba one day. They're going to declare Greenland part of US territory one day. This is the mindset right now. Grab what you can. They're absolutely going to do this. Jeffrey Sachs, professor, always a pleasure. videos.title: Irã divulga imagens bombásticas de helicóptero CH-47 Chinook americano destruído após impacto dir...
videos.description:
A mídia estatal iraniana divulgou imagens não verificadas alegando que um ataque com drone danificou um helicóptero CH-47 Chinook dos EUA no Kuwait. Relatos sugerem que a aeronave fazia parte de uma missão relacionada à queda de um caça F-15E Strike Eagle dos EUA em território iraniano. Um membro da tripulação teria sido resgatado, enquanto outro permanece desaparecido, e as operações de busca continuam. O Pentágono não confirmou as alegações. Com o aumento das tensões, o presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, classificou a situação como "guerra", sinalizando que não haverá trégua nas hostilidades. Os acontecimentos representam uma escalada perigosa no conflito em curso entre Irã e EUA.

#GuerraIrãEUA #AtaqueAoChinook #F15Abatido #ÚltimasNotícias #ConflitoNoOrienteMédio #ForçasMilitarEUA #AtaqueAoIrã #AlertaDeGuerra #Geopolítica #Trump

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A dramatic escalation in the Iran US conflict. Unverified images now claim a direct Iranian strike on a US military helicopter in Q8. Iranian state media outlet Press TV has released these images purportedly showing a damaged US Army CH47 Shinook at Camp Buring, a key American military hub in the Gulf. According to Iranian reports, a drone strike hit the aircraft, leaving the heavy lift helicopter severely damaged. The Shinook is a critical asset used to move troops, weapons, and supplies across the region. But there is a major catch. There is no official confirmation yet from Washington. The Pentagon has remained silent so far. Another Iranian outlet claims something even more striking. It says the helicopter was part of a search operation looking for a downed US fighter jet pilot. Because behind this incident lies an even bigger story. A US F-15E Strike Eagle has been shot down over Iran, marking a major turning point in the conflict. According to reports, one crew member has been rescued alive and now in US custody, receiving medical treatment, but the second crew member is still missing. Search and rescue operations are ongoing and time is critical. Meanwhile, tensions continue to surge. US President Donald Trump has responded bluntly, saying the loss of the aircraft will not affect diplomacy. His words were direct. This is war. Trump has also declined to reveal how the US would respond if the missing crew member is captured or harmed. That silence is raising new questions about what comes next. A major development is now emerging from the Iran war, marking a significant escalation in direct confrontation between the United States and Iran. According to American media, it is now confirmed that a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. US special forces have located one of the crew members. Multiple reports quoting sources say the aircraft involved was an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin seat fighter jet used for both airtoair and airto ground missions. The jet was carrying two crew members, a pilot and a weapons systems officer. Reportedly, both crew members successfully ejected after the aircraft was hit by Iranian fire. While one person was rescued alive from inside Iranian territory, search operations are still ongoing for the second crew member. In a significant operational decision, Israel reportedly cancelled planned strikes inside Iran to avoid interfering with the ongoing rescue mission. Iranian state media was the first to report that the US jet had been shot down, though it claimed the aircraft was F-35. State television in Iran also broadcast a message saying that anyone who locates the US personnel would be rewarded by the government. This marks the first confirmed instance since the start of the war that a US fighter jet has been downed by Iran's fire. The White House has acknowledged the development. Press Secretary Caroline Levit told Axios that the president has been briefed on the situation. The F-15E Strike Eagle manufactured by Boeing is a key component of US air power. It is designed to carry out deep strike missions as well as engage in aerial combat. This incident comes amid mounting losses in the conflict. Earlier in the war, three F-15E aircraft were reportedly lost due to friendly fire. The human cost is also rising. At least 13 American personnel have been killed since the start of the campaign. This comes after Donald Trump and his defense secretary Pete Hegseath have both repeatedly suggested that the US has effectively won this war. Hegsathth and military commanders have spoken of air superiority and how degraded Iran's air defenses are. This shows that Iran still retains capacity to defend its skies. As the search continues for the missing crew member and tensions remain at a peak, this latest incident underscores the growing risks facing US forces in an increasingly dangerous and expanding conflict. Iran downs US fighter jet captures its pilot amid war. US military's frantic search for captured pilot fails. Choppers deployed to trace missing F-35 fighter pilot. After Iran shot down a US F-35 fighter jet on Friday, a video circulated on social media showing American helicopters and planes searching for the missing pilot in southern Iran's Kistan. Iranian media also urged residents to capture the pilot dead or alive for a promising reward. Given that the American F-35 fighter J of the Zionist regime, the provincial police force issued an urgent statement announcing that an American Zionist enemy F-35 fighter jet had been targeted and subsequently shot down within the nation's airspace in the central region of the country. And given the potential for the pilot or pilots of this fighter jet to land in Kyiluier and Buoyer Ahmmed province, we earnestly request all members of the general public, including our esteemed nomadic communities and villagers to extend their full cooperation to the military and law enforcement agencies. To the esteemed residents of Kiluya and Buer Ahmad province, you, our beloved, honorable and fiercely patriotic people, I urge you, should you manage to apprehend any enemy pilot or pilots and successfully deliver them alive and unharmed into the custody of our military and law enforcement agencies, you will receive a valuable reward and a bonus for good news. Iranian IRGC linked Tasnim news agency claimed that Iranian forces foiled the US military's bid to rescue the downed fighter jet pilot. Iran's farce news agency shared video showing a purported missile attack on an F-15 air base in Israel's Hifa. The Iranian air defense systems also claimed to have successfully downed an American UH60 Blackhawk helicopter in Iraqi airspace, but the reports remain unverified. Later, Iran's Tasnim news agency claimed that the US military was trying to extract the fighter jet pilot whose condition remained unclear. The operation involved Blackhawk helicopters and a C130 Hercules aircraft. However, their efforts have so far been futile, the agency said. Washington has not commented on any of the claims regarding the alleged downing of a jet or the rescue mission. In March, an American F-35 had to execute an emergency landing after being struck by Iranian fire. Iran has been carrying out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military installations in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday vowed to crush Iran. Together with our American friends, we are continuing to dismantle the terror regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders. We are bombing uh bridges and we are striking their infrastructure. In recent days, our air force has successfully destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This is a truly significant achievement that effectively deprivives the revolutionary guards of both crucial financial resources and their capability to manufacture uh numerous weapons. In in full coordination between me and President Trump, between the Israel Defense Forces and the United States Army, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever. Israel is stronger than ever. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States air force capabilities, having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15, three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south, F18, one in south, F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. George has been asked to take early retirement from the post of Army Chief of Staff which he has held since August 2023. The development comes as a new report claimed that Trump administration officials were hiding the data related to US troops wounded or killed in the Middle East.

videos.title: 'DIA NEGRO PARA A FORÇA AÉREA DOS EUA': Irã ATINGE 4 aeronaves americanas; F-35 furtivo e Black H...
videos.description:
Dois helicópteros militares dos EUA foram atingidos por fogo iraniano na sexta-feira, enquanto realizavam uma missão de busca e resgate da tripulação de um caça abatido. Um helicóptero UH-60 Black Hawk da Força Aérea dos EUA e um segundo helicóptero de busca e resgate foram atingidos, mas conseguiram escapar, disse ao The Hill uma fonte familiarizada com o assunto, que falou sob condição de anonimato para compartilhar detalhes operacionais. Um assessor do Congresso, também sob condição de anonimato, disse ao The Hill que o Pentágono informou ao Comitê de Serviços Armados da Câmara dos Representantes na sexta-feira que o paradeiro do segundo militar abatido era desconhecido. As forças armadas iranianas anunciaram que suas forças de defesa aérea abateram pelo menos dois caças, cinco drones e mísseis na sexta-feira, descrevendo o ocorrido como um "dia negro" para as forças aéreas americanas e israelenses.

#guerradoIrã #IrãvsEUA #EstadosUnidos #notíciasaovivo #DonaldTrump #ForçasArmadasEUA #Hezbollah #IRGC #Teerã #F35 #Trump

A guy can take a mine, drop it in the water and say, "Oh, it's unsafe. It's not like you're taking out an army or you're taking out a country or you." He can drop it. Or you can take a machine gun from the shore and shoot a little few bullets at a ship. Or maybe an over the shoulder missile, small missiles. Uh, that's not for us. That'll be for France. That'll be for whoever is using the trade. But I think when we leave probably that's all cleared up today. I heard tremendous numbers of ships were sailing through. videos.title: Are Ships Breaking Out of the Strait of Hormuz | Report from A Ship in the Persian Gulf
videos.description:
On the April 2, 2026, update of the Strait of Hormuz, we discuss three ships running the southern Strait in Omani waters.  Does this signify a change in shipping? What is the US policy toward the Strait of Hormuz.  We talked with a merchant mariner who just returned from the Persian Gulf and what is it like onboard, what is the relationship with the US military, and why were cadets from the US Merchant Marine Academy still onboard ships in the region.  Finally, we look at the latest UNCTAD report on the impact of the closure.
